posts.author: Belal Sucks
posts.content:
Predictions: 6 of 14 Winners, 0 Perfect, 300 Points
|
Tied for 7404th

Strickland vs. Hernandez
Sean Strickland, DEC
Ive had the hardest time deciding who I think will win this fight. Their styles cancel eachother out so well on paper but I think what we'll learn here is that Stricklands grappling is underatted and Fluffys gas tank might be a bit over rated. Lets not forget that these guys are the saame age and from the same era as well. Aside from this past year I think most people would say Sean Strickland has been the better fighter with the bettter career thus far and I dont think Fluffy is leveling up but more so proving to people that he is higher level than a lot of the guys that were above him in the rankings. I think this is where he hits his ceiling though.

Osbourne vs. Idiris
Ode Osbourne, TKO, R2
Hard to count on Osbourne but oddly enough he's looked really improved in his last couple of fights. I honestly think you couldve given him the decision against Erceg based off damage. The clear gameplan to beat Ode is to take him down, but from the looks of things Idiris really likes to strike and doesnt have much of a submission game. That smells like an Osbourn KO to me.

Reese vs. Pereira
Zach Reese, SUB, R2
BAHAHA oh my lord if this happens for real throw this man Pereira in prison for life for fight fixing 🤣 he's gone so quickly from rising contender to actual can and now he's facing a highly fraudelent guy with an equally suspect gas tank and chin. On paper Michel should murder him in the first round and I bet you he'll be a decent sized favorite but Im telling you there is something fishy going on here so if you pick him DO NOT put hard earned money on it. I bet he takes a dive and/or gets finished by Reese here and the UFC cuts him.

Coria vs. Gurule
Alden Coria, TKO, R2
Luis Gurule *** are you even doing in the UFC bud?

Smith vs. Ko
Jacobe Smith, TKO, R1
Interesting Grappler vs grappler and prospect vs prospect matchup. I think Smith's physicality will end up being too much here. Hes got more strength and power and I think he will have a much easier time taking KO down than the other way around. KO has been put out before from strikes so I could see a KO/TKO from ground and pound as a real possibility. The longer this fight goes though the more Smith could find himself in trouble though as his gas tank looked pretty **** his last time out.

UFC Fight Night: Strickland vs. Hernandez

Sean Strickland vs Anthony Hernandez
Pick: Hernandez via decision
68% of Tapology users are picking Hernandez (68% decision)
Hernandez is 2.6 years younger (32.4 vs 35.0)
Hernandez opened at -198 on Draftkings and moved to -218
Hernandez opened at -245 on Fanduel and -250 on BetMGM
Strickland proj. +3.7 significant strikes attempted
Hernandez proj. +8.9 significant strikes landed
Hernandez proj. 8.08 takedowns landed (on 22.43 attempts) vs 0.91
Hernandez proj. 3.00 submission attempts vs 0.33
Hernandez proj. 36.1% finish chance vs 26.4% and 36.1% decision
Hernandez via submission has decent upside

Serghei Spivac vs Ante Delija
Pick: Delija via round 1 KO
90% of Tapology users are picking Delija (96% KO)
Spivac is 4.5 years younger (31.1 vs 35.6)
Both fighters have 78” reach
Spivac’s listed weight is 260 vs only 239 for Delija (+21 lbs.)

Delija proj. +21.1 significant strikes landed
Spivac proj. 2.11 takedowns landed (on 6.79 attempts) vs 0.00
Delija is a solid wrestler so Spivac likely won’t be able to control him enough
Spivac proj. 41.7% finish chance vs 37.5% and 20.8% decision
Delija has 12:6 KO ratio vs 7:4 (both have large KO downside)
3/4 times KO’d are in round 1 for Spivac
10/12 KO’s are in round 1 for Delija

Coria has gradually improved his footwork and striking selection over his last 4 fights. Sound fundamental offense. I've seen him get challenged in a couple of solid wrestling/grappling exchanges and do very well. Paces himself. Has finishing instinct.

Her progression over the last few years is great! That combo she landed on Priscilla was sweet. Cornolle will definitely be more defensively responsible, but she is prone to getting taken down. In their 1st fight, Nora was exiting her athletic prime, and Edwards was entering hers.

Costa vs Ige
Was gonna play Costa, but Ige is a guy I just can't bet against...Will need to be more cautious before making picks... no mulligan option.

posts.author: JeanSilvaEnjoyer
posts.content:
Predictions: 8 of 14 Winners, 2 Perfect, 475 Points
|
Tied for 3998th

Smith vs. Ko
Smith, T/KO, R1
Early pick, might change... I like Ko and think he's a solid grappler and striker for the unranked level of 170. I especially enjoy a grappler who goes for damage and doesn't just lay down and run out the clock, but I don't like this match-up for him. I thought at first that he was a good dog to bet on here because he has overperformed in his recent bouts whereas Smith underperformed last time around against Niko Price. Smith's cardio looked absolutely horrible there. He put a nasty beatdown on Price (who is at the tail end of his career as it is) and then started to gas badly before R1 was even halfway over. It was drastic. He was walking away from Price with his hands at his sides, Conor McGregor vs. Nate Diaz style. Even though Smith got the W, it was not the sort of performance everyone expected from a guy who was something like a -2500 favorite against 2025 Niko Price, who just got knocked out in less than a minute by retirement-fight Robbie Lawler. And maybe it's nothing, but the whole "JOE ROGANNNNN" thing on the mic makes me think Jacobe Smith might be a little touched in the head. Still, despite all of that, I think he beats Ko. For one thing, the fight with Price should have showed Smith and his team that his cardio needs major work, so he'll probably look better this time around. And even though I just said that he sounded touched in the head, the fact is the he showed some considerable fight IQ against Price. Price might be washed, but he's crafty, and Smith's ability to overcome unexpected adversity showed that he can make midfight adjustments. He gassed badly, yes, but he realized he was gassing, then paced himself well enough to recover stamina. And while he was recovering and put on the backfoot in the striking, he still was economical with his shot selection and retained enough power to sting Niko Price. Smith also latched up the submission over Price after a beautiful transition to the back on the mat. Submitting Niko Price is actually kind of impressive, even if it's old-as-hell Niko Price. Meanwhile, Ko has no submission wins on his record, so that's unlikely to be a factor here. Even though Ko has shown some skill and looked good, he has been the hammer for all 30 minutes of his 2 UFC bouts. Here, he will be the nail as soon as the bell rings. Ko was able to deploy the same game plan against Oban Elliott and Phil Rowe: take down, smother, and ground and pound. But if he can't ground-and-pound T/KO Oban or Phil Rowe, he's not going to be able to do it to Smith. And unlike Oban and Phil Rowe, Smith will not be so afraid of getting taken down that he turns into a statue on the feet.

* Edited at 01.07.2026, 10:36 PM ET *

Brahimaj vs. Soriano
Ramiz Brahimaj, SUB, R2
Brahimaj pulled out the underdog W over Vanderford in his last outing—he seemed to show some decent fight IQ with his ability to pressure Vanderford, land that nasty head kick, and (perhaps more importantly when it comes to predicting this fight) stay on his feet with his back to the cage when Vanderford tried to impose the wrestling. Meanwhile, Soriano gassed himself out by midway through R2 against Nikolay Veretennikov purely by laying on top of the guy and doing absolutely nothing. Maybe Soriano just had a bad weight cut or something going on health-wise in that fight, but his cardio seemed really poor and nothing he did really impressed. Soriano's R1 knockout over Uros Medic wasn't bad, but Uros almost got put away by Jose Medina in the exact same way, so I'm not going to rate his ability to penetrate the Doctor's impregnable defense that highly. I see Soriano being dangerous in R1 before fading and getting subbed.

Spivac vs. Delija
Ante Delija, TKO, R2
Spivac has a really tough time with guys with good hand speed and he gasses out after about 7 minutes in each fight. Thats a recipe for a very easy KO win for Delija in my opinion.

Ige vs. Costa
Melquizael Costa, DEC
Ah yes, the classic Dan Ige "are you good enough to be ranked" test. If history is any indicator of how this will probably go its either Ige wins by vicious KO or Melky wins a hard fought decision. Cant imagine Melky has this great 2025 run and then immediately loses all momentum against a journeyman but stranger things have happened.

Njokuani vs. Leal
Chidi Njokuani, TKO, R1
This is going to be an all out war for however long it lasts. I think stylistically I favor Chidi here just because we know already how Leal plans to fight him. He's gonna march forward with very little defense and swing from his shoes looking for a KO. People may not realize this because of his up and down results but Chidi is actually one of the best Muay Thai specialists in the UFC. His knees and elbows in the clinch are absolutely devastating and he uses that size and length to generate a ton of power. In theory, if Leal plans to maraud forward with low defensive IQ then he's more than likely going to walk right into one of those and not get back up. The biggest issue with Chidi though seems to be his chin, which is why I think a lot of people will be on Leal, but after the last performance from Carlos I cant say I trust his chin either. Gimme Chidi here all day.

del Valle vs. Leavitt
Yadier del Valle, SUB, R1
This is going to be as one sided as they make it. Del Valle has mega upside and can pretty much do everything well. The Dulgarian fight wouldve been the one to really open peoples eyes if it wasnt for the scandal behind it but I predict by the end of 2026 we will be looking at Del Valle as one of UFC's top prospects.

Judice vs. Miller
Carli Judice, TKO, R1
Oh my god this is going to be a murdering. If Im Juliana Miller Im pulling out of this fight by any means necessary.

Neal vs. Medic
Geoff Neal, TKO, R1
I like this matchup a lot actually. Feels like a good set up for a classic "Handz of Steel Neal" performance with the style of Medic essentially being kill or be killed. Neal can be tee'd off on himself as we've seen I just trust his durability a lot more. First or second round KO is pretty much a lock though.

Cornolle vs. Edwards
Joselyne Edwards, DEC
Edwards looks like she's hitting her stride since losing to Cornolle and Perez. The competition hasnt been super high level but she's finishing these girls which is something you need to do when UFC throws you a can. In this rematch I lean her here just because Cornolle is just sloppy and tough, no real skill set or advantage of hers to lean on. If Edwards is worth anything she should be able to redeem this one with a win this time out.

Brahimaj vs. Soriano
Ramiz Brahimaj, SUB, R2
Gotta be honest Im not too impressed with Puna despite the win streak. His last fight was so garbage and he managed to not even finish a guy with zero grappling even though he had like 10+ minutes of control time. Brahimaj on the other hand has really impressed me and hes slowly becoming one of my favorite prelim fighters. I think he's got an advantage big time on the ground and I favor him to get a sub in this spot.

Lebosnoyani vs. Rowe
Phil Rowe, TKO, R3
Taking Rowe here mostly for the hell of it. I know he struggles against grapplers but I think he's still a tough test for a guy whos unnproven making his debut. I was decently impressed with JPL on contender serries but when you really look deeper into some of this guys previous opponents its nothing too impressive. If Rowe can survive and not get subbed I think this kid gasses out and gets KO'd.

posts.author: Mothswords
posts.content:
Predictions: 7 of 14 Winners, 0 Perfect, 350 Points
|
Tied for 6887th

Strickland vs. Hernandez
Anthony Hernandez, DEC
I'm 29-8 (78%) on the 3 ufc cards so far this year, Incase you wanna fade my breakdowns or not. I don't think Strickland will be able to match the pace that fluffy sets. Fluffy is hungry to get a title shot and I'm not sure how motivated Strickland is. Also WE are fading Xtreme couture fighters

Neal vs. Medic
Geoff Neal, TKO, R1
Difficult fight to predict. Neal struggles against taller rangy opponents but I don't think Medic is the level of the opponents that Neal is losing to. I'd be shocked if this fight went longer than a round and a half. Someone is getting slept.

Reese vs. Pereira
Michel Pereira, TKO, R1
I hate to pick an extreme couture fighter but Zach reese ******* sucks. Michel Pereira has been a broken man since his loss to Fluffy, but if he loses this he should just retire

Smith vs. Ko
Seok Hyeon Ko, DEC
I believe seok hyeon ko is out of this fight, but if he isn't I think he can win by surviving a dangerous first round and grind out a decision on a gassed out smith.

Spivac vs. Delija
Ante Delija, TKO, R1
Wouldn't be shocked if spivac can takedown and sub Delija but I think Delija can avoid the grappling and light up spivac on the feet

Ige vs. Costa
Melquizael Costa, DEC
Costa is coming into his prime and has been building momentum. Ige is good but sometimes he just seems lost out there (I blame Xtreme couture)

Osbourne vs. Idiris
Ode Osbourne, TKO, R1
I'ma just say it, Kazakhstan fighters are the most overrated group of fighters in MMA.

Coria vs. Gurule
Alden Coria, TKO, R2
Coria looked great in his ufc debut. Gurule is a bum

del Valle vs. Leavitt
Yadier del Valle, SUB, R1
I'm still not sure how good del Valle is considering in his 2 ufc wins, one was against a bum and the other was against a guy who threw the fight. idk if I like leavitts drop to featherweight so I'm gonna fade it until I see how good he looks in the weight class first.

Njokuani vs. Leal
Carlos Leal, TKO, R2
Should be fun as long as it lasts. I'll never be confident picking Leal if he's gonna walk forward with his hands down but I still think he can break Chidi in this fight.

Judice vs. Miller
Carli Judice, TKO, R2
Should be a good ol fashioned ass whooping

Brahimaj vs. Soriano
Ramiz Brahimaj, SUB, R2
Ramiz has looked improved since his loss to themba. Puna can land a bomb but outside of that I don't think he'll be able to takedown and hold Ramiz, which is what he likes to do if he doesn't get an early ko.

Lebosnoyani vs. Rowe
Phil Rowe, TKO, R3
I'm gonna fade JPL making his debut against a guy who's not a complete bum. JPL is one of those guys who has good power and great grappling once it gets to the ground but he sucks at taking his opponent down and is very hittable. I could see JPL doing well early but gassing out and getting finished late in the fight

Tied for 6887th

Osbourne vs. Idiris
Ode Osbourne, TKO, R1
Why is ode the underdog against an 0-1 bum, I know his only loss is morales who would probably finish ode too but styles make fights and if this guy attempts to strike with ode, he’s getting chinned and obviously his wrestling isn’t next level because he’s been submitting and finished before, the triangle is odes best sub too. I think he chins idiris early in the 2nd or late in the 1st

Coria vs. Gurule
Alden Coria, TKO, R2
Gurule is no match on the feet and has good wrestling but won’t be able to utilize it because it’s not ufc level wrestling is like regional scene wrestling, dude wasn’t ready for the ufc

Cornolle vs. Edwards
Joselyne Edwards, DEC
Striker vs grappler matchup, Cornolle is well rounded but for the most part a wrestler and Edwards is just a striker with mediocre takedown defense but she’s been on a pretty good streak, if she can keep this on the feet she should beat cornolle, but if she can make it gritty along the cage and get takedowns I could see her winning, coin flip but I’ll take Edwards just because of the momentum

Brahimaj vs. Soriano
Ramiz Brahimaj, SUB, R2
Banger matchup, ramiz is pretty well rounded with some sneaky power and good submission game, and Soriano is a very agressive pressure wrestler with crazy power, odds are even which makes sense and I think ramiz takes is, he can get taken down but his jujitsu is heavily underrated and I think with Soriano shooting so much, ramiz will slip in another gilly finish

posts.content:
Predictions: 7 of 13 Winners, 1 Perfect, 405 Points

Honestly think Strickland puts a historic beating on Fluffy, if he doesn't finish him outright. Hernandez is not the wrestler everyone thinks he is, his takedowns usually meet firm resistance in the first round against good strikers with good TDD as he's soundly outstruck. The ONLY strikers with good TDD he's fought in Pereira, Kopylov and Shahbazyan are ALL consummate gassers. 3 wins over this archetype which favors his style have really boosted his perception it seems. Also:

- Allen is a middling striker at best, who chose to engage in repeated grappling exchanges though he had the advantage striking

- Dolidze was always a lumbering, sloppy striker and his reflexes had deteriorated significantly

Hernandez has great BJJ, but his wrestling won't be enough to take and keep Strickland down, and he'll find ZERO success with his rudimentary, moderately powerful striking where far more technical/wilder/more powerful strikers have struggled against Sean's defense. His real weapon is his pace, but this time he'll be facing someone who has far superior striking  but  can also hang  with the pace without gassing like the other strikers he fought before. Strickland also has great anti TD strikes (jab, teep)

* Edited at 02.10.2026,  1:31 AM ET *

posts.content:

Something to consider is that there is going into a major slump against pretty decently mid to high level opposition and having career worst performances, and then there is having a major step down in competition and still having everyone expect for you to be in that same form when you're significantly more skilled at MMA than your opponent.

I see it a LOT on the regional scene, guys will terrorize weaker competition and then **** the bed against a step up (everyone jumps off the support train here), then go right back to being a destroyer when they finally get a second to breathe and fight someone that isn't as skilled as their previous opponents.

That being said, I do not see Michel Periera losing to Zach Reese, Reese is one of the nicest guys on the roster but he is far from being on par with Michel's last 3 opponents.

Reese is most always a fun watch but he was getting brutalized in the clinch against Jackson McVey (who isn't UFC calibre as it stands), lost to Dusko (in my opinion), and beat Jose Medina -- None of those victories make me think he has what it takes to beat Demolidor.

Michel has been a shell of himself for sure, but again, that happens when you fight competition that is equally as skilled as you are.

Fluffy is not a bad loss, Abus is not a bad loss, even Kyle Daukaus (wrongfully released from UFC) is not a bad loss.

Both of these guys train at Xtreme Couture though, so I really do wonder if this fight will have a sparring pace.

Spivac vs. Delija
Ante Delija, TKO, R1
It might just be as simple as Delija Round 1 KO. in theory, Spivac could make this a pretty close fight by abusing the clinch and shooting endlessly, but it's one thing to have the tools, it's a whole other thing to actually have to intelligently utilize them. Spivac getting rocked by Almeida is a very bad look and I think Spivac's style works best when he's the one pressuring. I expect Delija to meet him in the pocket and he'll probably find the shot early.

Osbourne vs. Idiris
Ode Osbourne, TKO, R2
Ode's record is ugly but he's still a really explosive and powerful striker who has the ability to knock out anyone. Ode looked really good in his last fight against Erceg and was defending takedowns and avoiding getting controlled until the 3rd round. I also think Idiris' striking is pretty overrated, he's flashy but not that effective. I think Idiris does have a potential wrestling path, but his grappling doesn't look that great to me and Ode obviously knows thats a big hole in his game and I trust will keep improving in that area.

Coria vs. Gurule
Alden Coria, TKO, R3
I have always been a Gurule fader and I think this is the final straw for his UFC career. Gurule doens't really stand out to me in any area, and potentially has a suspect chin. Coria looked very impressive in his last fight and I feel like it's a similar matchup where Gurule will swing big and potentially mix in wrestling, but Coria is a great wrestler himself and on the feet he should be able to pick him apart and find a late finish.

Brahimaj vs. Soriano
Ramiz Brahimaj, SUB, R2
This is a perfect stylistic matchup for Ramiz. Puna slowed down significantly in his last fight and was allowing Veretennikov to snatch a few guillotines. I think you put Ramiz in some of those positions, he's finishing the fight. I think Puna will have success in the first round, but I think Ramiz will force bad shots with his improved striking and lock up a submission down the stretch.

posts.author: JeanSilvaEnjoyer
posts.content:
Predictions: 8 of 14 Winners, 2 Perfect, 475 Points
|
Tied for 3998th

Strickland vs. Hernandez
Anthony Hernandez, DEC
I was a Fluffy denier for a long time. I was a casual for a while so I didn't watch him vs. Pereira when it happened, and that fight should have clued me in to what Fluffy's about. Sean's TDD is very good, his striking is very good, but I think he's peaked. Maybe that's not kind to say. I was as hyped as anybody when he dethroned Adesanya, and that was not in any way a fluke victory. But between the two Dricus fights and the Costa fight, I haven't seen anything that makes me feel confident picking Strickland against a pressure wrestler. Yes, Dricus and Costa are both elite, but Sean looked like he was stagnating. The Dricus rematch was so depressing that Eric Nicksick went out in the media and trashed his own fighter, saying he wasn't fighting like he even wanted to be a champion again. Right or wrong, Nicksick's words signal to me that Sean is too stubborn to modify his style or prepare for new challenges. Fluffy's a new challenge. He's never fought anyone like Fluffy, and I don't think Sean's ready for it.

Neal vs. Medic
Geoff Neal, TKO, R1
I rate Medic's Salikhov win highly. I picked Salikhov as the dog in that fight, and we all saw how that turned out. Brutal KO. That's what Medic does. He either chins you brutally in R1 or gets brutally chinned in R1. But Neal's not chinny. The Prates KO was a picture-perfect spinning elbow. Could Medic put the straight left down the pipe and KO an aging Geoff Neal who's had more than his fair share of serious health complications? Absolutely. But Medic has shown real holes in his defense with the knockdowns to Semelsberger and Urbina on top of the KO to Punahele Soriano. Neal struggles with tall fighters, but he's a great striker himself, and Medic simply has not ever competed at this level. At the much lower level where he has completed, Medic has shown incredible power but also incredible vulnerability. And if you can get chinned by Puna, you can get chinned by Geoff Neal. Geoff's had some real ups and downs in his career, but it says something that Ian Garry was scared to death of this dude and had to smother him to a split decision. Not to mention that he put on one of the greatest fights of all time against Shavkat. Geoff might be over the hill, but not to the point where I'm going to fade him in favor of Medic.

Ige vs. Costa
Melquizael Costa, DEC
Melky was my boy from day one. Watching him get nearly murdered by Steve Garcia was horrifying, but it was sweet to see how he turned that devastating loss into a huge winstreak. I picked Charriere in his last fight because nobody knocks out Charriere, but Melky landed perhaps the second most beautiful head kick of 2025 and became the first guy to do it. The Ige test is a real thing, but Costa is more well-rounded than a guy like Sean Woodson, who's overreliant on his reach advantage. It's possible that Costa fades like he did to Erosa and gets starched by Ige, but I don't think so. Ige didn't look great against an aging Pitbull, and that was on a full camp, if I recall correctly. Costa is coming into his prime. I don't know if he's ready for more ranked guys at featherweight, but I suppose that's why the Ige test exists in the first place.

Spivac vs. Delija
Ante Delija, TKO, R1
I don't rate Spivac highly enough to beat Delija. Spivac has succeeded in this division because modern heavyweights have seriously lackluster grappling, but that's not true of Delija. Delija has massive power and good TDD, and I think that's all he's going to need here. That overhand right TKO by Waldo was nasty, but it was a very weird chain of events. Eye poke or not, Delija had Waldo in real trouble early in that fight, and Waldo has shown he's one of the more talented heavyweights in the UFC right now (take that for what it's worth). The amount Delija is fighting shows me that he is really hungry. He's one of like five dudes in this division who is truly skilled. Spivac is not ready for it. I think Spivac is more of a coach now than a fighter, anyway.

Reese vs. Pereira
Michel Pereira, TKO, R1
I'm picking Pereira... but I do NOT like it. Pereira has been a monumental disappointment. Yes, his antics were always more flash than substance, and yes, he was never going to be a top contender, but how do you get out-cardio'd by Abus Magomedov? And how do you get chinned in one punch by Kyle Daukaus? Granted, Daukaus has looked new and improved, but still, that should have been a lay-up for Pereira. The Abus loss was honestly more depressing than the Daukaus one. At least you can chalk the Daukaus one up to getting caught, but the Abus one was like watching a completely different man in the cage. If he doesn't style on Reese here, he's going to get cut. Reese, on the other hand, has racked up a couple of wins, but it's at the bottom of the division: Medina, Todorovic, and McVey, who are all on the chopping block... but Pereira's also on the chopping block. To be honest, Reese is not as bad as everyone makes him out to be. He's rangy, he's got BJJ, and that McVey fight was sloppy, but it showed that Reese (who signed up on very short notice) has real heart and some decent fight IQ. He got brutalized in the clinch in R1, which is where McVey is most dangerous, but in R2, Reese adjusted his gameplan, got the takedown, and found the submission... Pereira is washed. There is no doubt about it. And Reese is in his home territory, coming off of a couple of wins, but Pereira used to make this level of middleweight fighter look like light work. And even though he clearly can't do that anymore, I want to believe he can at least get a win at the bottom of the division. Plus, he's been training with Strickland, and that's got to mean something for his cardio and striking defense. Right? Right? Right? ...

* Edited at 02.12.2026,  4:41 PM ET *

posts.author: JeanSilvaEnjoyer
posts.content:
Predictions: 8 of 14 Winners, 2 Perfect, 475 Points
|
Tied for 3998th

Njokuani vs. Leal
Carlos Leal, TKO, R1
Toss-up. But Chidi's getting up there in years, and Leal might have cleaned things up just a tad since the Salikhov loss. Maybe. I'm not confident in Leal's skillset, but he will put Chidi on the backfoot, and Chidi is a better hammer than he is a nail. He's been TKO'd or KO'd 5 times in 36 fights. Leal has been TKO'd once in 29 fights. That's gotta mean something. Everyone wants to call Leal a fraud because he got clowned on by Salikhov, but it's not like this dude is Tallison Teixeira where he stinks it up the first time we see him fight for more than five minutes. We've seen him take on a decent fighter in Rinat and look good doing it. Hopefully it wasn't the sauce, though... But I'm not gonna overthink this one. If Chidi comes in with a strategic weight miss, though, I might be inclined to switch up.

Osbourne vs. Idiris
Ode Osbourne, TKO, R1
Very tough one to call. I like Ode. I thought he looked good against Erceg. He nearly KO'd him in the first, and he stuffed 7/10 takedowns for the rest of the fight. Obviously, he lost, but he showed up on 11 days' notice. Admittedly, that was at 135, not 125, but it was still against one of the top 15 flyweights in the world, a guy who nearly beat Pantoja. There's a lot of not-nice things to say about Ode. His record is ugly. His grappling is poor. He struggles to make weight. His cardio is a constant worry. But he is a very powerful, very slick striker. Alibi is also a base striker, but I don't think he can outstrike Ode. He hasn't shown that he's on that level. He got obliterated by Joseph Morales. Yes, Morales would also obliterate Ode Osbourne in very similar fashion, but all Idiris really has to show for himself are flashy regional highlights. Can he get takedowns and win the fight on the mat? That's possible, especially once Ode starts to fade, but if Ode can give Erceg a run for his money with the takedowns, then I think he can do the same on a full camp against Idiris. I just don't trust this dude at a UFC level. He's flashy with the spinning stuff, but I don't think he's going to be the better striker, and I'm not impressed enough with his physicality or his grappling to pick him. If this goes into the second round, though, I'm going to start to worry.

Coria vs. Gurule
Alden Coria, TKO, R2
One guy has shown he's ready for the UFC, and one guy has really struggled to show that.

Cornolle vs. Edwards
Joselyne Edwards, DEC
Cornolle lost to Rosa, a striker, by getting held to the mat for three rounds. She looked like dogwater in that fight. I don't rate Edwards's last three wins that highly, but I think she's shown levels of improvement that Cornolle has not. Plus she's 6 years younger.

Lebosnoyani vs. Rowe
Jean-Paul Lebosnoyani, DEC
Phil Rowe lost two rounds to Ange Loosa before Loosa gassed and Rowe smashed his face. If Rowe lost that fight, he would have been cut. He's very physically gifted, but he has not shown an ability to win based on those physical gifts. JPL's win on DWCS was a KO against a tall, rangy guy, and while I don't think he's KO'ing Rowe, I think the DWCS win shows that JPL is not going to be overwhelmed by the reach disadvantage.

del Valle vs. Leavitt
Yadier del Valle, SUB, R1
Leavitt isn't that bad, but he's struggled at lightweight, and the move down to featherweight is suspicious to me. It's not often that we see a guy move down from 155 to 145 and find great success. It's usually the other way around. I think Del Valle is better than Leavitt at Leavitt's thing, which is grappling, and Leavitt's shown in the fights against Paddy and Chase Hooper what happens when he goes up against someone who's a better grappler than him.

Judice vs. Miller
Carli Judice, TKO, R1
Execution

posts.author: film
posts.content:
Predictions: 7 of 14 Winners, 0 Perfect, 365 Points
|
Tied for 6751st

I have worked 4 of Josiah Harrell's fights (the last four aside from the most recent), and I can confidently say that I have no clue how he'll look in the UFC, but I do not think it will be very good.

He struggled REALLY badly against Kegan Gennrich, I actually came away from that fight more impressed with Gennrich than I did with Harrel despite giving the decision to Harrell due to his power shots, there were times during that fight where I was truly just spinning my pen around in my hand wondering if Harrel was going to get active or keep getting ate alive on the outside.

Harrell slowed really badly in that fight after 7 minutes and the range of Gennrich was giving him absolute hell, he also couldn't hold him down to save his life.

Seems to throw a lot of single shots and rely on power shots to win the fight rather than putting combinations together to get inside and initiate the grappling, he does have very good Ground and Pound though.

Know his game decently well as he trains over the bridge in Ohio with Matt Brown and them, He's a nice dude, but I can't confidently say that he will succeed in the UFC unless he makes some major changes to his game and starts to get an uptick in competition.

I didn't take much away from the Guillard win, that was a layup, QPJr doesn't take fighting seriously just trains at a local gym out of Central Kentucky so that was another layup and the same can be said for Mike Roberts. It is extremely easy to be 11-0 on the Ohio/Kentucky regional scene, we have next to no legitimate talent in this region. Budka was legitimately a talent that I was excited about when he got signed, and we saw how that went.

Harrell hasn't faced any high level competition until his last time out with LFA, but I can't get the Gennrich fight out of my head, I remember thinking the whole time "JDM would have a murder charge, UFC staff saved this dude"

If he steps in against Jacobe Smith, I'm very much looking forward to see how the similar stature affects the fight, I do not think Harrell stands a chance against a rangey fighter of UFC calibre.

Fighters "score" with grappling.  Sub attempts of course, but also transitioning to better, more dominant positions.  It's basically just lay-n-pray and wall-n-stall which are worthless.

"Damage based scoring" doesn't really capture how it really is.  It's more like "finish-oriented scoring".  The whole point of a MMA fight under the Unified Ruleset is for one fighter to stop their opponent.  It doesn't state that outright, but it's very much implied.  Judges know this, and so do refs.  Joe Rogan still hasn't caught on.

Another thing worth considering about Strickland-Fluffy is that if it goes to a competitive decision (which is a strong possibility considering Sean's fight style), then Sean's going to be at a disadvantage. Between the Cannonier fight and Sean vs. DDP 1, the judges have given him two highly questionable decision losses, and there was that one time where a judge inexplicably ruled 49-46 for Costa when Sean beat him for at least 4/5 rounds. Given (1) Sean's history with judges, (2) the fact that these are Texas judges, and (3) the UFC's desperation to court Mexican fans, Fluffy has a decent chance to win a decision even if he loses the fight.

posts.author: Anonymous
posts.content:
Predictions: 10 of 14 Winners, 3 Perfect, 600 Points
|
Tied for 840th

Strickland vs. Hernandez
Sean Strickland, DEC
I totally get why Fluffy is the favorite here and can definitely see him winning. He has cardio for days, can set a ridiculous pace, and can grapple his ass off. But I think this is a nightmare matchup for him. While I don’t think Strickland will be able to keep up with the pace Fluffy sets that well if he’s able to get to that point, I still think he can match Fluffy’s cardio if he can dictate how the fight goes which I think Fluffy will allow since he usually has trouble early on. If Strickland is able to gain any sort of momentum in round 1, it’s usually hard to stop it later on. Fluffy’s striking is also just not it at all. I know Strickland’s striking seems basic and not very diverse, but the point is that it works. He has amazing defense, an annoying teep kick, and a very well-timed jab. He’s also one of the better boxers in this division. And on top of that, he also has solid takedown defense. It’s not necessarily the greatest I’ve ever seen, but it is pretty solid. And he can offensively grapple pretty well too when he chooses to use it. He’s also really good at getting back up pretty much immediately after he gets taken down. If Fluffy can’t control Strickland on the ground imo he’s ******. I’m going to trust the underdog here.

Neal vs. Medic
Geoff Neal, TKO, R1
This is literally just a battle of who can crack who first. I have to lean Neal here though. Medic is very powerful and quick, but he lacks defense and doesn’t really have a good chin. I know Neal just got slaughtered by Prates’s elbow, but he usually has a solid chin and carries a lot of power himself. And while he isn’t as fast with his hands as Medic is, he’s way more tactical with his approach and will look for the right moments. Medic will throw the ******* kitchen sink at you and try to take your head off with every shot he even thinks about throwing. While those shots that he throws are very fast, you can easily see them coming. Even if they are straight shots he tends to telegraph quite a bit. I think Neal will chin him with a counter shot as he comes in.

Ige vs. Costa
Melquizael Costa, DEC
Going Costa here. Ige did not look good at all in his last fight. He just looked flat. I’d even argue he looked pretty flat in his previous fight to that as well. I think Costa will be faster on the feet although a bit less technical. And I think he’ll have moments on the mat as well. Welcome to the rankings Melquizael Costa.

Spivac vs. Delija
Ante Delija, TKO, R1
Spivac is honestly a disappointment. His striking is slow and sucks, and his takedowns aren’t even that good. He has great ground control for sure as well as decent submissions, but in terms of getting the fight down he is just not good at it. And on the feet, he is going to get decimated here. Delija hits hard and will capitalize on any good moment he has. And he’s a solid striker too. He was actually giving it to Waldo (who’s a top level HW striker all of the sudden) on the feet for pretty much the entire time before the whole incident occurred. I don’t like this fight for Spivac at all as I don’t really think he’s that good in the first place. Especially on the feet that is. Give me Delija 1st round TKO against the cage.

Smith vs. Harrell
Jacobe Smith, TKO, R1
Josiah Harrell is basically just a more exciting Torrez Finney. Way too short for the weight class but has more muscle than the entire world’s strongest man competition. That said, Jacobe Smith should win this fight pretty easily. Harrell just hasn’t impressed me. He’s put on decent performances, but it’s against cans and the performances that he is putting on aren’t exactly the most stellar. He even struggled and slowed down against Genrich who’s just not that good at all. He seems to be a decent grappler, but not great at it. Smith will probably outwork him in that area if he has to go there and is a way better striker. And not to mention, Harrell took this on short notice. And of course I had to accidentally press the go button.

* Edited at 02.15.2026,  3:34 PM ET *

posts.author: Anonymous
posts.content:
Predictions: 10 of 14 Winners, 3 Perfect, 600 Points

Reese vs. Pereira
Michel Pereira, TKO, R1
Michel has looked in awful form recently, but Reese just flat out sucks. Both are powerful, both are quick, both are chinny, both have **** cardio, but I think the difference here is Pereira’s athleticism. I think he can catch Reese unexpectedly whereas I don’t think Reese can do the same to Pereira. Because of that I am going to lean toward him, but if he isn’t taking it seriously which something in my gut seriously tells me isn’t, then Reese should win. But I’m going to trust Pereira one last time. What a fall off though.

Njokuani vs. Leal
Carlos Leal, TKO, R1
This is honestly a tricky one. Both prefer to strike and have solid power. They’re both also quite chinny. But I think I’m going to take Leal here. I just think the physicality is going to make that much of a difference. Leal literally has more juice in his system than the entire Tropicana corporation. I think he can use his massive size advantage to force Njokuani backwards and from there just kind of bully him similar to how he did Alex Morono. Chidi does not like to go backwards and will tense up in the moment if you make him which is basically what Leal does. I got Leal by standing TKO in this one.

Osbourne vs. Idiris
Alibi Idiris, DEC
Talk about a mid-off. I think Idiris gets it done. Ode actually didn’t look that bad against Erceg, but that was really only because of a few shots that he landed. Particularly in round 1 which is the round that Erceg always loses. But outside of that, he just really hasn’t impressed me even as of late. He KOd Gurule sure, but Gurule is honestly a can. And that’s actually Ode’s only win in his last 5. Against decent competition though. And while it is mainly due to grappling he has lost a lot of moments in striking too. And I think Idiris is a better striker than Ode so I’m going to take him here. Not high on him either though he looked like a bum against Morales.

Coria vs. Gurule
Alden Coria, TKO, R1
Gurule’s a can and Coria actually impressed me last time out. I actually decently rate Alessandro Costa. Taking him. Goodbye Gurule, next!

Cornolle vs. Edwards
Joselyne Edwards, DEC
Going Edwards as she’s looked better recently, but genuinely couldn’t care less.

Brahimaj vs. Soriano
Ramiz Brahimaj, SUB, R1
Soriano has looked solid since he’s moved to welterweight but I think his run ends here. I think he’ll be winning and then get club and subbed. Seems to be the story of Brahimaj’s fights nowadays.

Lebosnoyani vs. Rowe
Jean-Paul Lebosnoyani, DEC
JPL masterclass

del Valle vs. Leavitt
Yadier del Valle, SUB, R1
Del Valle’s a better grappler and they’re both mainly grapplers. Don’t need to explain how this one goes.

Judice vs. Miller
Carli Judice, TKO, R1
Judice by slaughter.
