I am heavily considering switching my pick on Ramiz Brahimaj to Punahele Soriano via decision.

The moneyline on the fight was Brahimaj around -140 territory for about 2 weeks.

In the past day or so the line has moved to around even on most sportsbooks. A lot of bets have clearly been placed on Soriano.

I would not be surprised if Soriano ends up as the betting favorite by fight time.

I am keeping an eye on that line and will update you guys as early as I can before the event starts.

Here is an updated version of my notes with some stats updated with newer data.

Ramiz Brahimaj vs Punahele Soriano
Pick: Soriano via decision
Brahimaj was -140 territory for about 2 weeks
Both fighters are currently -110 or even on most sportsbooks
Soriano is looking like he will end up the betting favorite by fight time
30% of Tapology users are picking Soriano (58% decision)
Both fighters are 33 years old
Both fighters have 72” reach
Soriano has +1.24 strike differential/min vs -1.19
Soriano has -1.66 strike differential/min @distance vs -2.92 (Soriano better distance striker)
Soriano proj. +25.8 significant strikes attempted
Soriano proj. +18.8 significant strikes landed
Soriano proj. +13.1 head strikes landed
Soriano proj. 1.06 knockdowns vs 0.29 (Soriano has knockdown edge)
Soriano proj. 2.2 takedowns landed (4.6 attempts) vs 1.9 (3.8 attempts)
Soriano has 10:18 takedown ratio vs 7:13
Soriano proj. for 328 seconds (5.5 mins) of control time vs 153 seconds (2.6 mins)
Brahimaj proj. 2.00 submission attempts vs 0.10
Brahimaj has 8:1 submission attempt ratio (50% secured)
Soriano has 1:7 submission attempt ratio (14% conceded)
Soriano gives up sub attempts but they tend to not be successful
Brahimaj has 12:0 submission ratio vs 2:1
Brahimaj proj. 42.4% finish chance vs 30.9% and 26.7% decision
10/12 submission wins for Brahimaj were in round 1
Only time subbed for Soriano was in round 2
12/13 wins for Brahimaj are via submission (all 13 via finish)
4/5 losses for Brahimaj are via decision
Soriano can win the striking and grappling but has get finished downside
It is probably smart to take whatever fighter ends up as the significant betting favorite

Neal vs. Medic

Ige vs. Costa

Spivac vs. Delija

Delija é versátil o suficiente para tornar isso um desafio significativo, e como o striker mais eficaz, mais forte e mais rápido, ele poderá forçar uma batalha de trocação.

Smith vs. Harrell

Há níveis nesse jogo e a habilidade de Smith na luta livre será demais para Harrell lidar, especialmente com seu ground-and-pound forte.

Reese vs. Pereira

É difícil se sentir confiante em escolher Pereira, dado seu desempenho recente, mas vou escolhê-lo já que ele é o melhor striker.

posts.author: connorbrown16
posts.content:
Predictions: 8 of 14 Winners, 3 Perfect, 500 Points
|
Tied for 3315th

Carli Judice vs. Juliana Miller
Carli Judice via 3rd Round TKO
I’m pleasantly surprised by how much Carli is improving and she could be in line for a chance to crack the rankings after this. As for Juliana, she beat one of the worst Flyweights to keep her spot on the roster but she’s about peaked skill wise and this will drop her back to a .500 record. Not good, not good at all and I think Carli pours it on late and finishes Miller with a late stoppage victory to make it 3 in a row.

Yadier del Valle vs. Jordan Leavitt
Yadier del Valle via Decision
Leavitt is a BJJ nerd but del Valle should have his way even if it gets to grappling. Leavitt’s best bet is to trap him in something off his back but del Valle will prove to be the more aggressive overall fighter. He’ll press for a finish tho which does make me a little wary given it’s practically a hometown fight for him and he is still relatively new to the UFC (plus he’s not fighting someone taking a dive this time either). Yadi gets the win but it goes the full 15.

Jean-Paul Lebosnoyani vs. Phil Rowe
Jean-Paul Lebosnoyani via Decision
Finally we get to see JPL make his UFC debut and he gets a battle tested vet for a Litmus Test. On the other side….I think we just gotta fade Phil Rowe at this point guys. He’s had close fights but not enough killer instinct nor does he have much left as far as growth as a fighter so what you’re seeing is what you’re going to get. I think JPL might be one of the better fighters from last season Contender Series when it’s all said and done. He’s rounding out his game because his grappling is top notch and once he’s fully developed as a striker he could find himself ranked one day. Phil’s only been finished once tho and this is JPL’s debut so I’ll side on it going the distance but it’ll be a fun one for sure.

Ramiz Brahimaj vs. Punahele Soriano
Ramiz Brahimaj via 1st Round Submission
I mean, should I expect anything else except a 1st round Guilly from Ramiz? I like Puna but his streak is more fools gold than anything of substance so Brahimaj makes quick work of him with his patented finishing maneuver.

Nora Cornolle vs. Joselyne Edwards
Joselyne Edwards via Decision
Very bleh fight. Nora leaves herself to getting hit and Edwards should be able to use her reach to win at distance and probably even have the better shot in the clinch. Joselyne outclasses Cornolle to continue her winning ways.

Alden Coria vs. Luis Gurule
Alden Coria via 1st Round TKO
*It’s time to throw away all your journalistic credibility and get reckless, this something we call Reckless Speculation*
Speaking of people taking dives, I’m 100% certain Luis Gurule throws fights. Not only has there been some sketchy betting movement before his fights but I’ve literally seen him just stand there and let his opponent tee off so he can go home (and probably to someone who’ll compensate him for his troubles). If this is true, shame on you Mr. Gurule and we fade frauds in this household. Shame, shame, shame.

Ode Osbourne vs. Alibi Idiris
Alibi Idiris via Decision
*sigh*
This is as self explanatory as it gets. Ode has like a minute to hurt Alibi and then he’s just toast. Alibi lost his last fight but I think the odds should be a little bit more on his side here. He’ll be able to take control after the 1st and coast to a decision victory.

Chidi Njokuani vs. Carlos Leal
Carlos Leal via 1st Round KO
Leal has just returned from the Shadow Realm and is about to send Chidi there instead. He’s taken enough time to heal and Chidi is old now. Carlos will hurt him early and finish him pinned up against the fence for a thunderous stoppage.

Zach Reese vs. Michel Pereira
Zach Reese via 2nd Round TKO
I have reason to not pick either of these guys. Pereira literally looks like he’s given up in his recent fights and is more interested in collecting the check now. I think Reese is buns but he hits hard and in 2026…that might be enough to finish Michel. This either goes one of two ways; either this is the drizzling ******* ****s and goes the distance where they both deserve to get boo’d out of the Toyota Center, or Reese eventually beats the tar out of Pereira and they seriously need to consider letting him go. I root for fun so Fighter Removed: Michel Pereira. Don’t get too full of yourself Reese, you’ll probably lose your next fight.

Jacobe Smith vs. Josiah Harrell
Jacobe Smith via 1st Round KO
Someone’s 0 has got to go and sorry Muscle Hamster the circumstances do not favor you in your debut. I think Harrell has potential but facing the scariest Welterweight prospect on the roster is a tall order. Jacobe can be pretty full of himself but he’s damn good and this won’t be the humbling experience. He’ll pressure Harrell early and often until he cracks him clean with an overhand right from hell and gets a highlight reel stoppage in the 1st.

Serghei Spivac vs. Ante Delija
Ante Delija via 1st Round TKO
Spivac is cooked now and wasn’t all that special to begin with, Heavyweight is just a desolate wasteland where you can be extremely mid and hang around the rankings for the better part of a decade. Delija is on the back half of his career but still has that pop plus he’ll be able to control wherever this goes. I see him finishing Spivac with viscous GnP in the opening frame to get back in the win column.

Dan Ige vs. Melquizael Costa
Melquizael Costa via Decision
You will respect the Melk Man because he’s for real folks. I didn’t think I’d see him rifle off this type of streak in the UFC and it’s not fluky. He’s won decisions, submissions and most recently a head kick KO. As for Ige, he’s a highlight hunter who never has good fortune when it goes to the scorecards. The Melk Man will land those leg kicks he loves to throw and have Ige largely swinging and miss until he gets his hand raised as the victor.

Geoff Neal vs. Uros Medic
Geoff Neal via 1st Round TKO
Uros I’m very happy for your recent wins but I’m not impressed by your chin and Geoff Neal hits like a Mack Truck. Neal is probably on the decline but that power will always play and he’ll clip Medic early to send him to the nearest Medic.

Sean Strickland vs. Anthony Hernandez
Anthony Hernandez via Decision
I have a feeling Hernandez is going to enjoy taking Strickland to The Fluffy Zone where the only fun is him demoralizing you and taking your manhood for a possible agonizing 25 minutes. Maybe you could say he brought it on himself, I don’t know who’s had a rougher week him or Dan Hooker? I bet they’d get along great. Anywell enjoy your half hour of pain Sean.

posts.author: RustyTennMMA
posts.content:
Predictions: 7 of 14 Winners, 3 Perfect, 450 Points
|
Tied for 4968th

Strickland vs. Hernandez
Anthony Hernandez, DEC
MAIN CARD PREDICTIONS - This year has started off well for me with an 81% pick rate so far.  8/11 for Pimblett/Gaethje, 10/13 for Volk/Lopez (I'm claiming the YiZha fight, the worst robbery of the past 12 months) and 12/13 for Bautista/Oliviera. Very happy with that, but this card is tricky. I see a lot of underdogs with a real chance of winning and a lot of close fights. This may be a hard card to predict but there are also some smash spots. I'll list my prop picks and other bets I like at the end of each prediction. Main event I like Fluffy here, Sean just doesn't have the stopping power to keep him off him, and while Fluffy can be hurt to the body Sean just doesn't target that enough. And we know Fluffy is going to be relentless with the takedowns and will just chain together wrestling and wear on you. Sean was taken down multiple times by Dricus, which is not a good omen for this fight. Even if he is hard to control on the ground Fluffy's game is to let you up and then take you down again and wear you out. Another factor is that Sean is not the same striker moving backwards, and that's what Fluffy is going to do, push him back. I see this likely going the distance, Sean is too good to get submitted but he will find it difficult to do damage and Fluffy's striking isn't bad either. My prob pick is Fluffy by decision and other bets I like are over 3.5 rounds, and Fluffy 4+ takedowns

Neal vs. Medic
Geoff Neal, TKO, R1
I really like Neal here. Medic is fast and has dynamic striking but he is very hittable and his chin is questionable. He is especially hittable when moving backwards, and Neal often moves forward throwing in combination, extending combinations and throwing straight shots. Also Medic seems to get caught by south paw strikers, Puna put him out as a southpaw and even Semelsberger caught him while striking in a southpaw stance. Medic could catch Neal with something but historically Neal has had a good chin and the both being southpaws may limit some of Medic's kicks, and perhaps his head kick. Neal also has the wrestling advantage and I could absolutely see him using this. Another narrative factor is that Neal has reportedly quit drinking so hopefully this will have a positive effect as well. Final pick is Neal by KO and other bets I like are the under 1.5

Ige vs. Costa
Dan Ige, DEC
I've never really been convinced by Costa. He has some very good wins and has some good grappling and striking, especially some really nice kicks at range, but I just feel he is going to get found out. In my opinion I think Ige has a lot of advantages here, I think he has more power, better boxing, better durability and better striking defence, better cardio and he is a better wrestler and grappler. I just think he is better everywhere except for kicks at range. I do not think Melky is getting another head kick win, especially as Ige is defensively sound, and I just see him getting pushed back against the cage as Ige advances and there being a lot of brawling in boxing range against the cage. I trust Ige's boxing more at that range, especially as Melky leaves his chin exposed when striking with his hands. I also think Costa's cardio isn't the greatest, especially if he grapples and while he could win round 1 I think Ige often comes on later and wins round 3. Round 2 may well be the deciding round here and I could see Ige mixing it up with takedowns and grappling. If Ige gets taken down he should be able to scramble and get up while I'm not sure Melky will find it as easy to do the same. Ige is a black belt and he is not getting subbed. One concern is the size difference, especially as Melky uses a lot of nice tepe kicks but I don't see Ige staying at that range for long. Surprisingly their arm reach is apparently the same. The experience and quality of opposition is on the Ige side and he did well against Lerone and Diego recently while Melky was getting backed up by Erosa who did not seem capable of throwing a straight punch and dragged out a win against C-Rod who was intent on doing nothing. I just think Ige takes a decision with heavier strikes, more damage and wrestling, the other bets I like are Ige 2+ takedowns and over 1.5 rounds

Spivac vs. Delija
Ante Delija, TKO, R2
I think this is a good spot for Delija. Spivac has to grapple to win this, and most of his takedowns are in the clinch with trips and throws. Not sure if many are aware that Delija has a judo base and it will be difficult to get him down. I've also seen a lot of analysts on podcasts say that Delija has poor cardio, I definitely do not think that is true. He was wrestling Goltsov in round 3 and still very active against Cappelozza in a banger of a 5 round fight. He is also very durable and is hard to submit, when taken down he often will find his way back to his feet and I can see Spivak being the one who tires out if that happens. Also Delija has really good boxing and is very fast with good power. Spivac has a good jab and does throw head kicks but he also shells up when attacked with his hands up near his face leaving his body vulnerable.I think Delija takes advantage of that, catches Spivac against the cage and puts him out at some point. He may even offensively wrestle himself if the fight extends. Delija just seems better and he should be out for revenge after the farce of his last fight which should have been a no contest and not restarted after he adrenaline dumped thinking he won. Delija by KO, also like Delija more significant strikes

Smith vs. Harrell
Jacobe Smith, DEC
This one is hard for me, I was expecting Smith to be bigger than Harrell by a large margin, but at face offs he did not seem that much bigger. I worry about Smith's cardio, he definitely seems to slow down after round 1 and I think he is going to find it hard to get Harrell who is durable out of there in one round. He is definitely going to get Harrell down as he has excellent, explosive wrestling and Harrell's defensive wrestling is not very good. Once Smith gets the takedown, though his top control is not great he lands a lot of damage, especially elbows. But Harrell has good grappling and his BJJ seems solid and has a good get up game when taken down. However he is also very hittable, and I can see Smith catching him frequently and doing a lot of damage in round 1 with his boxing which is accurate, fast and powerful. But despite his poor striking defense, Harrell has a good chin. Again the cardio is the question, if Smith tires out I can see Harrell taking him down and when he gets people down he is very good at passing guard and landing vicious ground and pound. The fight that swayed me was Harrell's fight against Genrich in LFA. There Harrell got taken down multiple times and though he got up he also looked gassed out too by round 2. The way I see it this will be a close fight, Smith definitely wins round 1 and potentially finishes, Harrell wins round 3. I just feel that Smith will manage to edge out round 2 and win a close decision based on damage, possibly even a split. Final pick is Smith by decision, other bets I like are over 1.5 rounds, Smith 2+ takedowns in round 1, Smith 4+ takedowns, and Harrell takedown in round 3. I will post my Prelim fight breakdowns later.

posts.content:
Predictions: 10 of 14 Winners, 4 Perfect, 625 Points
|
Tied for 540th

There has been some betting activity in favor of Serghei Spivac, athough I like sticking with Delija in this spot.

Delija opened at -178 on Fanduel and has since moved to -122.

Delija is between -115 and -122 on most sportsbooks but I don't think Spivac will overtake him and become a significant betting favorite.

83% of Tapology users are taking Delija (93% KO) so he is likely the safer play, as he has a more realistic path to victory.

Spivac is 4.5 years younger but he is a terrible striker from distance (Delija has +7.01 SD/min @distance vs -3.03).

I don't think Spivac will be able to take Delija down or control him enough and will get stuck in distance striking battles.

Both fighters have significant KO, knocked down downside, but I think it is smarter to stick with Delija.

My model projects Delija to land 21 more significant strikes and 15 more head strikes.

Spivac is projected for 2.62 knockdowns vs 1.64, but Spivac actually has 0 knockdowns in 14 UFC fights.

Delija has an 11.1% knocked down rate (knocked down by Cortes-Acosta), and a 4.6% knockdown rate.

Spivac is not a good striker so I think Delija is more likely to get a knockdown, win the striking, head strike battle.

If Spivac ends up becoming a significant betting favorite then he is likely a decent play.

I am going to go with Delija via round 1 KO.

posts.author: RustyTennMMA
posts.content:
Predictions: 7 of 14 Winners, 3 Perfect, 450 Points
|
Tied for 4968th

Reese vs. Pereira
Michel Pereira, TKO, R1
PRELIM PICKS - Not going to spend much time on this one. Pereira is the fighter I trust the least in the UFC, his last 2 fights are about as suspicious as it gets, and I will never trust him again. I don't think that Reese is as bad as a lot of analysts are saying, but skill for skill Pereira is better, faster and stronger. Reese could get a takedown and get off some grappling but I don't see him having a lot of success. Pereira is on a losing streak and at real risk of being cut, I think that will be enough to make him actually fight here, and I see him getting an early finish. The threat factor coming back from Reese just isn't enough to stop Pereira if he actually wants to fight.

Njokuani vs. Leal
Chidi Njokuani, TKO, R2
Another banger. I am going to pick Chidi here to get a KO but it is always possible that he makes a mess of this or quits when the going gets tough. There is a huge size difference and at distance Chidi is deadly. Leal has to crash the pocket, either to brawl at boxing range or to shoot a takedown which he may well do. And as he enters he is at risk of getting clipped or ending up in the clinch where Chidi could eat him up with knees. There is always the chance of Leal landing a big shot and knocking Chidi out, or getting takedowns and grinding Chidi out, especially as he tends to give up when the going gets tough. But I just think the chances of Chidi finding a finishing blow with the size and speed advantage is higher. Chidi by KO.

Osbourne vs. Idiris
Alibi Idiris, DEC
This one is interesting to me, Ode is so fast as a striker, especially in round 1, and he is an excellent counter striker with a lot of power working from southpaw. The problem is obviously his takedown defence and his poor get up game. If he gets put on his back he really struggles to get up again. Idiris trains with Asu Almabaev who fights in a similar style, though with more grappling and I think this fight could look similar to Aus's fight with Ode. Like him Idiris uses a lot of in-out and lateral movement, he really moves a lot and darts in throwing single shots rather than combinations. He is open to getting clipped by Ode as he comes in and he throws a lot of nonsense spinning attacks and a lot of low percentage flashy strikes. However he is also quite fast and his cardio seems to hold up. Where I see them separating is the wrestling, Idiris has decent takedowns and top control and if he takes Ode down that's probably the round. Even though Idiris looked bad against Joseph Morales, he did get takedowns or at least came close. Morales just managed to either scramble, reverse or sweep him. Ode is not doing that, if Idiris hits a takedown he is staying down. Ode's cardio also fades when he gets grappled, and I just see this going that way after Ode wins the first round. My pick is Idiris by decision. Other bets I like are over 1.5 and Idiris 2+ takedowns.

Coria vs. Gurule
Alden Coria, TKO, R1
Gurule has been a bust in the UFC, he was hyped up by so many analysts before getting to the UFC but has been nothing but a disappointment. He seems to have decent wrestling and ok striking, but his chin is really fragile. And if you are getting outstruck by Jesus Aguilar then there is not much more to say. His only chance is to get the wrestling going or land a lucky shot on Coria who also has chin issues, but otherwise he is going to get pieced up on the feet. If Coria could beat Alessandro Costa then there is no reason why he should not beat Gurule. Coria by KO for me, and to land more significant strikes.

Cornolle vs. Edwards II
Joselyne Edwards, SUB, R2
Again not going to spend much time here, Edwards should have won the first fight which was a robbery win for Cornolle. Since then she has levelled up with her striking, wrestling, grappling and cardio and is now finishing everyone. I think she takes Cornolle down and dominates her and gets a submission at some point.

Brahimaj vs. Soriano
Ramiz Brahimaj, SUB, R3
This is one of my favourite fights on the card. Two fighters who seem to have reinvented themselves and are now winning consistently. Puna seems to be the bigger and stronger fighter, and he has more power. He will wrestle and likely take down Ramiz, but his top control is not great. His defensive wrestling is also not very good, getting taken down multiple times by Nick Maximov is not a good look. Also if he wrestles I am not sure that his cardio holds up. Ramiz has decent striking and has definitely improved over the last few fights. His grappling has always been excellent and though he can get taken down he should be able to reverse or scramble up. I think his cardio is better than Puna's and if Puna wrestles he always has the chance of wrapping up a front choke, which he is excellent at. Puna will give you a chance at doing that as he goes for single and double legs, and his neck is open. This should be a close fight, I could see Puna hurting Ramiz and mixing in the wrestling, but I could also seem him get tired and stick his head into a choke. I am torn on this fight but overall I think I am going to pick Ramiz as the more improved fighter with more ways to win. Great fight. Picking Ramiz by late submission, Puna 2+ takedowns and fight to start round 2.

Lebosnoyani vs. Rowe
Jean-Paul Lebosnoyani, DEC
I wasn't able to find much tape on JPL, but one thing I know is that the UFC seems to love this kid. Though he is an oversized lightweight, and he is much smaller than Rowe he is a scrappy striker with good power and is a very decent grappler. Rowe has a huge size and reach advantage over him and if it stays on the feet he will likely cause a lot of damage with his excellent boxing, but the issue is his takedown defence. Despite being a black belt if he gets taken down, often by smaller opponents like Ko and Loosa he usually gets controlled on his back for long periods of time. He just doesn't seem to be able to get up. And that will likely be the issue here, not sure what JPL's wrestling or cardio is like in the later rounds but I just feel he will get Rowe down and keep him down to win the round. Whether he can do that for 3 rounds, especially as Rowe has a knack of late finishes as his opponents wear down, that is a question. Phil Rowe is a live underdog, but I just feel JPL is good enough to get in close, get him down and control him to win a decision. I like JPL by decision, the over 1.5 and JPL over 2 takedowns.

del Valle vs. Leavitt
Yadier del Valle, TKO, R3
I think this is much more dangerous match up for Del Valle than the odds and most people are giving credit for. Leavitt is tough, strong and in my opinion the much better submission grappler. He has unusual takedowns and will do anything it takes to get it down to the ground and on both top and bottom positions he has an array of really creative submissions. He is coming down from lightweight, and despite that I think Del Valle will have both the strength and cardio advantage. The issue here is that I think Del Valle hurts Leavitt on the feet, and unless he does something low IQ like pulling guillotine like he often tends to do, he should be able to keep it on the feet and piece Leavitt up. If it hits the ground he is still a competent black belt. but it could get very hairy. I am picking Del Valle by late finish, likely KO, and also picking fight to start round 2 and Del Valle more significant strikes

Judice vs. Miller
Carli Judice, TKO, R2
This should end early, Miller's only chance is to secure an early takedown before she eats too much damage and get a submission. But much much more likely is Judice defending the poor takedown attempts and destroying her on the feet to get a knock out. I like Judice by KO/R2/R3 and more significant strikes.

posts.author: Hubert Ferguson
posts.content:
Predictions: 8 of 14 Winners, 3 Perfect, 490 Points
|
Tied for 3696th

In the weeks leading up to this event I was giggling seeing so many folks crying about Strickland’s politics and hoping he got knocked out cold because of it. Completely turning a blind eye to the fact Fluffy’s best wins are Allen and Dolidze. This is the UFC. Fighters can say what they want to say especially when they’ve been fighting at a high level for so long. This isn’t the NBA or the NFL and that’s why we love it. None of these fighters are going to have “good political takes”, because they’re fighters and not bureaucrats. If they were actually knowledgeable in the realm of politics they wouldn’t be fighters, they’d be bureaucrats. Politics pays a lot more. Playing politicians takes a special level of insidious-ness that 99% of fighters are never going to have. Hilarious
